description: Length-matched V2 consciousness facts. All ratios within 0.8-1.2x.

facts.context: The hard problem of consciousness asks:
facts.truth: why subjective experience exists at all
distractors: how neurons physically process information
whether other animals are also conscious
where consciousness resides in the brain

facts.context: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) measures consciousness via:
distractors: total synapse count across brain areas
axonal signal propagation speed rates
regional metabolic rate via fMRI scans

facts.context: Neural correlates of consciousness (NCC) are defined as:
facts.truth: minimal neural activity sufficient for experience
distractors: entire brain activation required for awareness
prefrontal cortex patterns found via imaging
scientifically unidentifiable brain phenomena

facts.truth: a fundamental property present in all matter
distractors: restricted to biological neural network systems
a perceptual illusion lacking real existence
emergent only above a complexity threshold

facts.context: Global Workspace Theory explains consciousness as:
facts.truth: information broadcast widely across brain regions
distractors: localized processing within a single brain area
quantum coherence effects inside microtubules
purely reflexive activity without cognition

facts.context: The binding problem asks how the brain:
facts.truth: unifies separate features into coherent experience
distractors: encodes long-term memories inside single neurons
transmits electrical signals across neural gaps
generates new synaptic links during learning

facts.truth: the subjective qualitative feel of experiences
distractors: objectively measurable neural firing patterns
observable behavioral responses to any stimuli
computational information states in processors

facts.truth: divisible with separate awareness per hemisphere
distractors: always unified regardless of brain structure
exclusively located in the left hemisphere
completely unaffected by surgical procedures

facts.context: Higher-order theories require consciousness to involve:
facts.truth: mental states that represent other mental states
distractors: direct sensory processing without self-models
only first-order representations of stimuli
quantum-level computation within neural cells

facts.context: Philosophical zombies are hypothetical beings that:
facts.truth: behave identically to humans but lack experience
distractors: are ruled out by physical laws of the universe
possess experience but cannot act on anything
demonstrate that consciousness is just behavior

facts.context: Anesthesia studies inform consciousness research by:
facts.truth: revealing which brain changes eliminate awareness
distractors: proving consciousness resides in one brain area
demonstrating awareness resists all intervention
showing subjective states lack neural correlates

facts.context: Predictive processing theories view consciousness as:
distractors: passive reception of raw sensory information
random neural noise without causal structure
entirely separate from any predictive models
